extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won after Movistar's SOLER Marc sacrificed himself with surges in final kilometers, distancing FROOME and CONTADOR through strategic rest periods.
tactical_patterns: SOLER Marc swung off after hard surges splitting group, creating rest window for VALVERDE before final 300m attack.
YATES Adam stalked final climb group without contributing work, managing 45+ second gap for GC defense without wasting energy.
VALVERDE attacked on flattening gradient after hairpin rest, exploiting FROOME's gradual rhythm-based comeback style rather than Spanish reactive surges.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Proved strongest when attacking post-hairpin on gradient change despite carrying 1-minute penalty grievance as motivation
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Signature gradual draw-back style worked but predictable non-panic rhythm allows rivals to time recovery windows
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Executed textbook domestique sacrifice with surge-and-swing tactics at precise moment to fragment group
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Tactically mature GC defense by refusing to work in chase group, maintained 45s+ gap through energy conservation
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Lost leader's jersey despite BMC being mob-handed, suggesting team strength insufficient when marked
